<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=19024>

Created attachment 3639
  --> <https://bugs.r-project.org/attachment.cgi?id=3639&action=edit>
Proposed implementation of R_mapEnv.

The goal here is to provide a C API for iterating over bindings in a primitive
R environment, without exposing internals of the environment itself.

text:
*[Bug 19025] New: Provide tool for manipulating order of objects on search path*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=19025>

            Bug ID: 19025
           Summary: Provide tool for manipulating order of objects on
                    search path
           Product: R
           Version: R-devel (trunk)
          Hardware: Other
                OS: Other
            Status: NEW
          Severity: normal
          Priority: P5
         Component: Low-level
          Assignee: <mailto:R-core@R-project.org|R-core@R-project.org>
          Reporter: <mailto:kevinushey@gmail.com|kevinushey@gmail.com>

Created attachment 3640
  --> <https://bugs.r-project.org/attachment.cgi?id=3640&action=edit>
Patch implementing .reattach tool.

It can occasionally be useful to rearrange items on the search path, without
explicitly detaching and attaching those environments. I've attached a patch
implementing a tool, `.reattach`, that can be used to move an existing object
on the search path from one location to another.

The primary motivation here is to allow "safe" re-parenting of environments in
this specific context, without needing to expose things like ENCLOS and
SET_ENCLOS more broadly.

<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=19026>

Created attachment 3641
  --> <https://bugs.r-project.org/attachment.cgi?id=3641&action=edit>
Initial pass at an API.

The intention here is to have a mechanism to allow for applications embedding R
to evaluate R code without tripping the debugger.

text:
*[Bug 19027] New: Provide before / after hooks for install.packages(), for R front-ends*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=19027>

            Bug ID: 19027
           Summary: Provide before / after hooks for install.packages(),
                    for R front-ends
           Product: R
           Version: R-devel (trunk)
          Hardware: Other
                OS: Other
            Status: NEW
          Severity: normal
          Priority: P5
         Component: Wishlist
          Assignee: <mailto:R-core@R-project.org|R-core@R-project.org>
          Reporter: <mailto:kevinushey@gmail.com|kevinushey@gmail.com>

This is mainly a request for RStudio, but would be useful more broadly for
other applications embedding R...

It can be useful for applications embedding R to know when `install.packages()`
is being invoked. For example:

- On Windows, the front-end could notify the user if they're attempting to
install a package that is already loaded / in use, and that installation will
likely fail.

- The front-end can also prompt the user to take some action that could help
installation to succeed.

- After installation is complete, a notification could be used to tell the
front-end to take some action (e.g. rebuild the Packages pane).

Having explicit hooks here would also allow R to differentiate between errors
that occur in the hooks provided by the front-end application, and errors that
occur in the install.packages code itself (and could make that clear in error
reports). It would also make it unnecessary for RStudio to mask these functions
to attach its own behaviors.

My proposal here is to have two hooks, specified via R options
`before.install.packages` and `after.install.packages`, which get invoked on
enter / exit.

A similar set of hooks would be useful for `remove.packages()`.

`update.packages()` appears to delegate to `install.packages()`, so would also
be handled by the above scheme.
